description: Era theme system (v1.4.0). A second setting in setup.html ('Appearance') to pick a YouTube-era THEME, orthogonal to light/dark. TWO AXES: data-theme=era, data-mode=light|dark (every era gets both palettes). Four eras: 2005 Original (Verdana/Arial, 0px radius, plain), 2009 Classic (Arial, ~2px, red/gray retro chrome), 2014 Flat (Roboto, ~2px, flat white + red), 2021 Modern (Roboto, 12px, current look). ARCHITECTURE (the point — clean + extensible): (1) expand CSS design tokens beyond colors — add --font-family/--logo-font/--radius/--radius-lg/--shadow/--density/--accent; (2) each era = one [data-theme=era] block with structural tokens + light palette, plus a [data-theme=era][data-mode=dark] color override; (3) a JS theme REGISTRY array driving both the picker UI and the switcher (add a theme later = one CSS block + one registry entry); (4) theme-scoped overrides via [data-theme=x] .el {} for rare bespoke bits. Fonts: 2005/2009 = system (Arial/Verdana), 2014/2021 = bundled Roboto (no new fonts). Persist era+mode in localStorage (two keys); migrate old 'theme'=light/dark into {era:default, mode:that}. Apply across index/setup/watch via extended initTheme; the header 🌙 toggle stays and flips data-mode orthogonally. Everything additive/graceful. Significant two-reviewer QA required; Design must nail token architecture, attribute model, registry, migration.

history.note: Feature initiated via /kickoff. New feature era-themes (Era Theme System, v1.4.0). Before bootstrapping, finalized the prior shipped feature: docs/exec-plans/active/media-session-hardening.md was a stale tombstone for already-shipped v1.2.1 work. Copied it to docs/exec-plans/completed/media-session-hardening.md with a tombstone header and a 'Shipped v1.2.1' Progress-log entry (acceptance criteria checked off). This toolset cannot true-`mv`, so the active copy remains as a tombstone to be `git rm`'d by the main loop. For context, v1.2.2 'native-transport' and v1.3.0 'ui-polish' also shipped since then as lightweight, non-pipeline changes with no exec plans — that is fine and expected. Checked for overlapping active plans: none (docs/exec-plans/active/ holds only the media-session-hardening tombstone + .gitkeep). Tech-debt tracker left untouched (Closed #1 unbounded transcode-cache remains intact; Active table empty); era-themes does not overlap it.

Bootstrapped and staged for discovery. Slug era-themes, v1.4.0 minor feature adding a YouTube-era THEME system orthogonal to light/dark (two axes: data-theme=era + data-mode=light|dark). Description captured verbatim in state, including the explicit architecture mandate (expanded CSS design tokens beyond colors, one [data-theme=era] block per era + a dark color override, a JS theme REGISTRY driving picker + switcher, theme-scoped bespoke overrides), the four eras (2005 Original / 2009 Classic / 2014 Flat / 2021 Modern), the fonts decision (system for 2005/2009, bundled Roboto for 2014/2021, no new fonts), localStorage persistence with migration of the old 'theme'=light/dark key into {era:default, mode:that}, and application across index/setup/watch via an extended initTheme with the header 🌙 toggle flipping data-mode orthogonally. Flagged: significant two-reviewer QA required; Design must nail token architecture, attribute model, registry, and migration. Artifacts reset to null (fresh feature — no requirements/design/exec_plan yet). Awaiting /prep-pm-discover to route the product-manager. BOOTSTRAP ONLY: no discovery/design content produced and no specialist agents invoked this turn.

Discovery complete. Wrote docs/exec-plans/active/era-themes.md, grounded in a read of the live token system (public/css/style.css :root/[data-theme=dark], ~25 hardcoded border-radius values across the file, 3 hardcoded font-family declarations), initTheme/toggleTheme in public/js/common.js, and header/setup-page structure across index.html/setup.html/watch.html. Requirements cover: token expansion (--font-family, --logo-font, a --radius/--radius-lg scale, --shadow, --density, --yt-red/accent) with an explicit no-regression AC for the 2021/light+dark refactor; the two-axis attribute model (data-theme x data-mode, structural+light tokens under [data-theme=era], dark override under [data-theme=era][data-mode=dark]); a JS theme registry as single source of truth for both the setup.html Appearance picker and the switch logic (5th-theme-is-mechanical AC); persistence via two localStorage keys with migration of legacy theme=light/dark, recommending 2021 Modern as the default/migration era (justified: it IS today's shipped palette, so it's the only zero-regression choice) with explicit fail-safe behavior for invalid/corrupted stored values; early/pre-paint theme application to avoid FOUC on all 3 pages; system-font stacks for 2005/2009 vs bundled Roboto for 2014/2021 with no new font files; namespaced per-theme bespoke overrides. Testability section extracts a pure resolveTheme(storedEra, storedMode, legacyTheme) helper as the one node:test-covered piece (7 enumerated cases), explicitly scoping the rest as manual/visual verification (theming can't be judged by automated tests) rather than deferring test coverage that CONTRIBUTING.md would otherwise require. Risks flagged, ranked: token find-replace regression (top), FOUC, invented dark palettes for eras with no historical dark mode, header-toggle semantic narrowing to mode-only, migration edge cases. Explicit out-of-scope: >4 eras, per-page theming, auto-switching, and dynamic <meta name=theme-color> updates (flagged rather than silently assumed). No conflicts found with active exec plans (none exist) or the tech-debt tracker (Closed #1 is unrelated). Set artifacts.requirements and artifacts.exec_plan to docs/exec-plans/active/era-themes.md; artifacts.design remains null pending principal-engineer. User confirmation of the normalized requirements was not yet solicited in this turn beyond the detailed steering already provided in the discovery prompt itself; recommend the Engineering Manager/orchestrator surface the plan for a quick user sanity check before design, per the standard confirm-with-user step.

Design complete. Appended a '## Technical Design' section to docs/exec-plans/active/era-themes.md (replacing the Design placeholder) and set artifacts.design to that file. Grounded in a full read of public/css/style.css (every :root/[data-theme=dark] token + all 28 border-radius, 4 box-shadow, 3 font-family declarations, enumerated by line), public/js/common.js (initTheme/toggleTheme + the getStarRating/resolveChannelName export-shim), all three HTML pages, and test/unit/star-rating.test.js for the node:test pattern. Design specifies: (1) Token architecture -- structural tokens (--font-family/--logo-font/--mono-font/--radius/--radius-lg/--density/--shadow/--shadow-lg) under [data-theme=era]; palette tokens (colors incl. --yt-red) light under [data-theme=era] + dark under [data-theme=era][data-mode=dark]; :root kept as a 2021-light fail-safe default; full CSS skeleton given. Introduced --shadow-lg (in addition to --shadow) so the modal keeps its stronger elevation with zero regression. A line-by-line find-replace table maps 20 border-radius -> var(--radius) (small chrome) or var(--radius-lg) (thumbnail/player/artwork/modal); EXTENDED the exec plan's 'perfect circles exempt' rule to stadium/pill shapes (.skip-btn 27px, .speed-badge 14px) so 2021 skip/badge chrome stays pixel-identical; flagged the two intentional 2021 rounding deltas (.audio-artwork 8->12, .modal-content 4->12) for QA sign-off. --density applied narrowly to .sidebar-item and .video-info (2021 8px == current, zero regression). (2) Concrete CSS for all 8 palettes (2005/2009/2014 light+dark; 2021 == today verbatim), era-authentic, with bespoke namespaced overrides (2005 underlined links, 2009 glossy .btn gradient). (3) Pure resolveTheme(storedEra, storedMode, legacyTheme) with exact logic + 7 node:test cases (target: test/unit/resolve-theme.test.js). (4) applyTheme/initTheme/toggleTheme(mode-only)/setTheme(era) with keys ft-era/ft-mode + legacy 'theme' migration. (5) Inline <head> FOUC bootstrap (self-contained mirror of resolveTheme) for all 3 pages. (6) THEME_REGISTRY {id,name,year,blurb,swatch:[c1,c2]}. (7) setup.html Appearance picker HTML/JS/CSS. (8) Test list (1 automated + manual visual checklist). (9) Ordered 10-step task breakdown (tokenize -> 2021 regression checkpoint -> other eras -> JS/registry -> FOUC -> settings UI -> tests). Alternatives (per-era stylesheets, single combined key, prefers-color-scheme) rejected with rationale; performance impact nil. Flagged for EM/QA: the top regression risk is the border-radius find-replace (mitigated by line table + a mandatory 2021 light+dark regression pass BEFORE any new era lands) and resolveTheme/inline-bootstrap sync. Stage advanced discovery -> design. Ready for Engineering Manager to slice the task breakdown.
